REPLY TO DEFENDANT FAST WRAP RENO ONE LLC'S OPPOSITION TO ALTEN CONSTRUCTION INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (TRANSACTION ID # 62378924) (TRANSACTION ID # 62378924) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.THE COURT NOTES PRIOR TO ARGUMENT THAT THE FOLLOWING LANGUAGE FROM ITS TENTATIVE RULING IS STRICKEN AS ERRONEOUS: "... A FOOTNOTE 44 IN CRAWFORD STATES THAT SECTION 2782 DOES NOT RELIEVE AN INDEMNITOR FROM ITS DUTY TO DEFEND IN THE INDEMNITY CONTEXT." THE MATTER IS ARGUED BY THE PARTIES. THE COURT CONTINUES THE HEARING ON THIS MATTER TO OCTOBER 2, 2018 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. THE COURT PERMITS THE PARTIES TWO ROUNDS OF SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING. THE FIRST ROUND OF SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING IS DUE 9/7/18 AND IS TO ADDRESS WHAT IS THE LEGAL STANDARD FOR DETERMINING WHETHER A DUTY TO DEFEND CLAIM IN AN UNDERLYING CASE WHERE THE DUTY TO DEFEND UNDER 2778 IS ENCOMPASSED BY A DUTY TO INDEMNIFY A CONSTRUCTION DEFECT CLAIM; WHETHER IT IS ENHANCED BY, A POSSIBILITY OF, OR SOMETHING ELSE. THE SECOND ROUND OF SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING IS DUE ON 9/18/18 AND IS TO ADDRESS WHAT TO DO IF THERE IS CONFLICTING EVIDENCE THAT THERE IS A DUTY TO DEFEND AND EVIDENCE THAT THERE IS NO DUTY TO DEFEND, AND WHETHER THAT REQUIRES A DETERMINATION UNDER THE LAW THAT THERE IS A DUTY TO DEFEND OR A DENIAL OF A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BECAUSE THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT TO BE DECIDED BY THE TRIER OF FACT. THE COURT INDICATES THAT FOR THE FURTHER HEARING ON THIS MOTION, DUTY TO INDEMNIFY IS NOT AT ISSUE, AND THE ONLY ISSUE IS WHETHER DEFENDANT FAST WRAP RENO ONE, LLC OWED PLAINTIFF ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC. A DUTY TO DEFEND THE CLAIM OF THE DISTRICT IN THE UNDERLYING LAWSUIT THAT WAS NEVER FILED.JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT FAST WRAP RENO ONE, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE PLAINTIFF ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS DENIED. FAST WRAP HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS IMPROPER BASED UPON THE FACTS PLED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE SUBCONTRACT AT ISSUE IN THIS ACTION, WHICH IS ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT A TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, CONTAINS A PROVISION THAT BROADLY STATES: "[FAST WRAP] SHALL DEFEND, INDEMNIFY, AND SAVE HARMLESS [SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT AND ALTEN]'FROM ANY AND ALL CLAIMS, DEMAND, CAUSES [OF] ACTION, DAMAGES, COSTS, EXPENSES, ACTUAL ATTORNEY'S FEES,'OF EVERY KIND AND NATURE WHATSOEVER'ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH [FAST WRAP'S] OPERATIONS TO BE PERFORMED UNDER [THE SUBCONTRACT]'." (COMPLAINT, EXH. A., P. 4.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLENE P. KIESSELBACH, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: GORDON AIAVAO, CSR #11216, GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG (302/CPK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Daniel A. Serot, Tel. No. 1-415-291-8844 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Richard E. McGreevy of the Law Offices of Leach & McGreevy LLP, Tel. No.
1-415-775-4455 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - Plaintiff Alten Construction, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication on the issue that defendant Fast Wrap One, LLC had a duty to defend Alten is denied. Alten has not satisfied its summary adjudication burden of showing that there was a claim against it for which a duty to defend applied. After the hearing on August 28, 2018 which continued the hearing on Alten's motion to allow the parties to submit further briefing, the court contacted the parties and requested that their briefing also address the issue of whether the San Francisco Unified School District made a claim asserted against Alten for which a duty to defend applied. Alten's position that the District's demand made in correspondence is sufficient to trigger a duty to defend lacks merit. While the issue does not appear to have been squarely decided, this court determines that the phrase "defend actions or proceedings" in Civil Code section 2778(4) means a duty to defend does not arise until an adjudicative or quasi-adjudicative proceeding of some kind such as a court or administrative agency proceeding, internal grievance, or arbitration has been initiated by a claimant. This construction of section 2778(4) comports with the language of that section, case law construing that section, other case law regarding a duty to defend, and common understanding of a duty to defend. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Continued To Oct-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To Oct-02-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DANIEL A. SEROT, ESQ. of Van De Poel, Levy, Arneal & Serot, LLP (Tel: 415-291-8844) appearing for plaintiff Alten Construction, Inc.;
Atty. RICHARD E. MCGREEVY, ESQ. of Law Offices of Leach & McGreevy LLP (Tel: 415-775-4455) appearing for defendant Fast Wrap Reno One, LLC.

PLAINTIFF ALTEN CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The Court notes prior to argument that the following language from its tentative ruling is stricken as erroneous: "... a footnote 44 in Crawford states that section 2782 does not relieve an indemnitor from its duty to defend in the indemnity context." The matter is argued by the parties. The Court continues the hearing on this matter to October 2, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. The Court permits the parties two rounds of supplemental briefing. The first round of supplemental briefing is due 9/7/18 and is to address what is the legal standard for determining whether a duty to defend claim in an underlying case where the duty to defend under 2778 is encompassed by a duty to indemnify a construction defect claim; whether it is enhanced by, a possibility of, or something else. The second round of supplemental briefing is due on 9/18/18 and is to address what to do if there is conflicting evidence that there is a duty to defend and evidence that there is no duty to defend, and whether that requires a determination under the law that there is a duty to defend or a denial of a motion for summary adjudication because there is a triable issue of fact to be decided by the trier of fact. The Court indicates that for the further hearing on this motion, duty to indemnify is not at issue, and the only issue is whether defendant Fast Wrap Reno One, LLC owed plaintiff Alten Construction, Inc. a duty to defend the claim of the district in the underlying lawsuit that was never filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FAST WRAP RENO ONE, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Fast Wrap Reno One, LLC's motion to strike portions of the plaintiff Alten Construction, Inc.'s first amended complaint is denied. Fast Wrap has not shown that the request for attorney's fees is improper based upon the facts pled in the first amended complaint. The subcontract at issue in this action, which is attached as Exhibit A to the first amended complaint, contains a provision that broadly states: "[Fast Wrap] shall defend, indemnify, and save harmless [San Francisco Unified School District and Alten]'from any and all claims, demand, causes [of] action, damages, costs, expenses, actual attorney's fees,'of every kind and nature whatsoever'arising out of or in connection with [Fast Wrap's] operations to be performed under [the subcontract]'." (Complaint, Exh. A., p. 4.) Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/CPK)
